children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Oil Shale, Tar Sands, and Other Strategic Unconventional Fuels Act of 2005”.

United States oil shale, tar sands, and other unconventional fuels are strategically important domestic resources that should be developed to reduce the growing dependence of the United States on politically and economically unstable sources of foreign oil imports;

the development of oil shale, tar sands, and other strategic unconventional fuels, for research and commercial development, should be conducted in an environmentally sound manner, using practices that minimize impacts; and
development of those strategic unconventional fuels should occur, with an emphasis on sustainability, to benefit the United States while taking into account affected States and communities.

children.heading: Leasing program for research and development of oil shale and tar sands
children.label: c Leasing program for research and development of oil shale and tar sands
children.text: In accordance with and any other applicable law, except as provided in this section, not later than 180 days after , from land otherwise available for leasing, the Secretary of the Interior (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) shall make available for leasing such land as the Secretary considers to be necessary to conduct research and development activities with respect to technologies for the recovery of liquid fuels from oil shale and tar sands resources on public lands. Prospective public lands within each of the States of Colorado, Utah, and Wyoming shall be made available for such research and development leasing.section 241 of title 30August 8, 2005

children.heading: Programmatic environmental impact statement and commercial leasing program for oil shale and tar sands
children.label: d Programmatic environmental impact statement and commercial leasing program for oil shale and tar sands

children.heading: Programmatic environmental impact statement
children.label: 1 Programmatic environmental impact statement
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , in accordance with , the Secretary shall complete a programmatic environmental impact statement for a commercial leasing program for oil shale and tar sands resources on public lands, with an emphasis on the most geologically prospective lands within each of the States of Colorado, Utah, and Wyoming.August 8, 2005section 4332(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Final regulation
children.label: 2 Final regulation
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the completion of the programmatic environmental impact statement under this subsection, the Secretary shall publish a final regulation establishing such program.

children.heading: Commencement of commercial leasing of oil shale and tar sands
children.label: e Commencement of commercial leasing of oil shale and tar sands
children.text: Not later than 180 days after publication of the final regulation required by subsection (d), the Secretary shall consult with the Governors of States with significant oil shale and tar sands resources on public lands, representatives of local governments in such States, interested Indian tribes, and other interested persons, to determine the level of support and interest in the States in the development of tar sands and oil shale resources. If the Secretary finds sufficient support and interest exists in a State, the Secretary may conduct a lease sale in that State under the commercial leasing program regulations. Evidence of interest in a lease sale under this subsection shall include, but not be limited to, appropriate areas nominated for leasing by potential lessees and other interested parties.

children.heading: Diligent development requirements
children.label: f Diligent development requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall, by regulation, designate work requirements and milestones to ensure the diligent development of the lease.

children.heading: Initial report by the Secretary of the Interior
children.label: g Initial report by the Secretary of the Interior
A
children.text: develop the program, complete the programmatic environmental impact statement, and promulgate the final regulation as required by subsection (d); and

children.label: B
children.text: conduct the first lease sales under the program as required by subsection (e); and
a schedule to complete such actions within the time limits mandated by this section.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary of Energy, in cooperation with the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Defense, shall establish a task force to develop a program to coordinate and accelerate the commercial development of strategic unconventional fuels, including but not limited to oil shale and tar sands resources within the United States, in an integrated manner.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Energy (or the designee of the Secretary);

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior (or the designee of the Secretary of the Interior);

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary of Defense (or the designee of the Secretary of Defense);

children.label: D
children.text: the Governors of affected States; and

children.label: E
children.text: representatives of local governments in affected areas.

children.heading: Recommendations
children.label: 3 Recommendations
children.text: The Task Force shall make such recommendations regarding promoting the development of the strategic unconventional fuels resources within the United States as it may deem appropriate.

children.heading: Partnerships
children.label: 4 Partnerships
children.text: The Task Force shall make recommendations with respect to initiating a partnership with the Province of Alberta, Canada, for purposes of sharing information relating to the development and production of oil from tar sands, and similar partnerships with other nations that contain significant oil shale resources.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: A Initial report
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Task Force shall submit to the President and Congress a report that describes the analysis and recommendations of the Task Force.August 8, 2005

children.heading: Subsequent reports
children.label: B Subsequent reports
children.text: The Secretary shall provide an annual report describing the progress in developing the strategic unconventional fuels resources within the United States for each of the 5 years following submission of the report provided for in subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate the creation and implementation of a commercial strategic fuel development program for the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: evaluate the strategic importance of unconventional sources of strategic fuels to the security of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: promote and coordinate Federal Government actions that facilitate the development of strategic fuels in order to effectively address the energy supply needs of the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: identify, assess, and recommend appropriate actions of the Federal Government required to assist in the development and manufacturing of strategic fuels; and

children.label: E
children.text: coordinate and facilitate appropriate relationships between private industry and the Federal Government to promote sufficient and timely private investment to commercialize strategic fuels for domestic and military use.

children.heading: Consultation and coordination
children.label: 2 Consultation and coordination
children.text: The Office of Petroleum Reserves shall work closely with the Task Force and coordinate its staff support.

children.heading: Interagency coordination and expeditious review of permitting process
children.label: k Interagency coordination and expeditious review of permitting process

children.heading: Department of the Interior as lead agency
children.label: 1 Department of the Interior as lead agency
children.text: Upon written request of a prospective applicant for Federal authorization to develop a proposed oil shale or tar sands project, the Department of the Interior shall act as the lead Federal agency for the purposes of coordinating all applicable Federal authorizations and environmental reviews. To the maximum extent practicable under applicable Federal law, the Secretary shall coordinate this Federal authorization and review process with any Indian tribes and State and local agencies responsible for conducting any separate permitting and environmental reviews.

children.heading: Implementing regulations
children.label: 2 Implementing regulations
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Secretary shall issue any regulations necessary to implement this subsection.August 8, 2005

children.text: are ready for demonstration at a commercially-representative scale; and

children.label: B
children.text: have a high probability of leading to commercial production.

children.label: A
children.text: technical assistance;

children.label: B

children.label: C

children.heading: National oil shale and tar sands assessment
children.label: m National oil shale and tar sands assessment

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a national assessment of oil shale and tar sands resources for the purposes of evaluating and mapping oil shale and tar sands deposits, in the geographic areas described in subparagraph (B). In conducting such an assessment, the Secretary shall make use of the extensive geological assessment work for oil shale and tar sands already conducted by the United States Geological Survey.

children.label: i
children.text: the Green River Region of the States of Colorado, Utah, and Wyoming;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Devonian oil shales and other hydrocarbon-bearing rocks having the nomenclature of “shale” located east of the Mississippi River; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any remaining area in the central and western United States (including the State of Alaska) that contains oil shale and tar sands, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Use of State surveys and universities
children.label: 2 Use of State surveys and universities
children.text: In carrying out the assessment under paragraph (1), the Secretary may request assistance from any State-administered geological survey or university.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To facilitate the recovery of oil shale and tar sands, especially in areas where Federal, State, and private lands are intermingled, the Secretary shall consider the use of land exchanges where appropriate and feasible to consolidate land ownership and mineral interests into manageable areas.

children.heading: Identification and priority of public lands
children.label: 2 Identification and priority of public lands
children.text: The Secretary shall identify public lands containing deposits of oil shale or tar sands within the Green River, Piceance Creek, Uintah, and Washakie geologic basins, and shall give priority to implementing land exchanges within those basins. The Secretary shall consider the geology of the respective basin in determining the optimum size of the lands to be consolidated.

children.heading: Compliance withsection 1716 of title 43
children.label: 3 Compliance withsection 1716 of title 43
children.text: A land exchange undertaken in furtherance of this subsection shall be implemented in accordance with .section 1716 of title 43
encourage development of the oil shale and tar sands resource; and
ensure a fair return to the United States.

children.heading: Heavy oil technical and economic assessment
children.label: p Heavy oil technical and economic assessment
children.text: The Secretary of Energy shall update the 1987 technical and economic assessment of domestic heavy oil resources that was prepared by the Interstate Oil and Gas Compact Commission. Such an update should include all of North America and cover all unconventional oil, including heavy oil, tar sands (oil sands), and oil shale.

children.heading: State water rights
children.label: r State water rights
children.text: Nothing in this section preempts or affects any State water law or interstate compact relating to water.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: s Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.
